1.content:
A quick look at my clock showed that I only had 5 minutes until my ride was here; so, I hurriedly finished packing the last of my camping equipment into the pack on my bed. I was packing some tampons, along with the rest of my toiletries, when there was a knock on my door. “Come in!”

Barely looking up from what I was doing, I saw my younger brother Eric walk in. He’d been avoiding me for most of the morning, still annoyed that I was able to do something he wasn’t. “How is it that you’re able to spend a week hiking the Appalachian Trail; but Mom and Dad won’t let me go to Comic Con by myself?”

“Maybe it’s because there’s fewer registered sex offenders in the woods.” Looking at Eric, I said, “Stop complaining Eric, you’ll have plenty of chances to go to Comic Con when you’re older.”

Rather than acknowledge me, Eric turned and left my room in a huff. The week-long hike I was packing for was something I’d been planning with my best friends and my boyfriend for the better part of the last two months. It was something we did every year before school started, the 4 of us would do something big together.

I was just finishing with my packing when I heard a car horn outside. Going to the window, I saw my best friend Melissa leaning out the back window of my boyfriend Kyle’s car. Grabbing my things off my bed, I started down the hall. Rounding the corner leading to the stairs, I saw my parents and Eric waiting at the front door.

I could already see the tears streaming down my mom’s face as I walked down the steps. “Geez Mom; you're always so emotional."

Hugging, Mom said, "I'm a mother, it comes with the territory."

Giving my Mom a hug, I said, “I’m only going to be gone a week, Mom; you’ll be picking me up at the airport before you know it.”

After saying goodbye to my Dad and Eric, I walked out the door and down the sidewalk. I barely made it 10 feet before Kyle was running to grab my things for me. I wanted to think that was him being a gentleman, but he wanted to make sure everything was neatly packed in the back.

Hopping into the car, I looked back at Melissa and Liz sitting in the back seat. I was about to say hello when I noticed the depressed expression on Liz’s face. Turning to Melissa, I said, “What’s up with her?”

As Liz turned to look out the window, Melissa said, “Tonya broke up with her last night; apparently she just wanted to see what it was like dating a girl.”

My smile faded as I turned to face Liz. I had my suspicions that Tonya wasn’t gay, but I’d kept those to myself. Placing a hand on Liz’s leg, I said, “It’s going to be okay, Liz.”

Turning to me, tears streaming down her face, Liz said, “That’s easy for you to say; at least your boyfriend is actually attracted to you.”

Having known Liz since the first grade, I knew when to leave her alone. Removing my hand from her leg, I turned back to the front as Kyle got into the car. Seeing the look on my face, he said, “I’m guessing you heard about Liz and Tonya?”

I nodded, but Liz interrupted me before I could say anything, “So help me; if this is all we’re going to talk about for the next week, I’ll get out right now.”

Looking back as he turned the car on, Kyle said, “Fine Liz, we promise not to talk about it anymore.”

*************************


The drive to our starting point took just over an hour; I hung back with Kyle, after unloading everything, as he called his parents to have them pick up his car. After ending the call with his parents, the two of us went to meet up with Melissa and Liz. We barely made it five feet before I felt Kyle’s hand on my arm. Turning around, I saw him staring at me, “Come on, we have to catch up with the others.”

“I know, but there’s something I want to talk about first.” Releasing my arm, Kyle paced around nervously, “What are the chances that we’ll finally be intimate?”

I groaned when I realized what Kyle was talking about; he’s been on my case for the last few months about taking our relationship to the next level. “I know you think you’re ready, Kyle; but I’m not.” Looking at him, I continued, “We’re about to start college; do you really want to ruin all that on by getting me pregnant?”

“No, I suppose you’re right.” Pausing, Kyle said, “Can we at least continue sleeping together?”

Unbeknownst to my parents, Kyle and I had been sleeping together for last 6 month. There had been a few times where we came close to being caught but managed to convince our families that we weren’t doing anything. “Sure, so long as we keep it a secret from our folks.”

With that, Kyle and I held hands as we caught up with Melissa and Liz. Liz wasn’t known for her patience; something made clear when she said, “Geez, what took you guys so long?”

“Sorry Liz, we were just having a talk.”

“Well come on; we’re 8 miles from camp and it’s going to get dark soon.”
1.title: Prepping For Vacation

11.content:
It took us just over an hour to reach our destination. After setting up the tents, I went off to relieve myself while Kyle got the fire started. I was looking for a good spot, close to the group but still far enough away to have some privacy, when I noticed a dim light nearby.

Quickly relieving myself, I ventured off towards the light; knowing full well there were no houses nearby or anything that could explain the light. Getting closer, I found myself standing in front of a narrow cave entrance. Feeling compelled to go inside, I was about to enter when I felt a hand on my shoulder, “What are you doing?”

Spinning around, I saw Kyle standing in front of me with Melissa and Liz. Looking at them all, I said, “I saw this light and followed it to this cave; I wanted to see what it was.”

Always the cautious one, Melissa stepped passed me and peaked inside the cave, “It’s pretty dark in this, Olivia; perhaps we should wait until morning?”
11.title: Setting up Camp

111.content:
I looked at the cave entrance as I pondered my decision; I couldn’t explain it, but I felt as if something was compelling me to go inside. Stepping towards the cave, I looked back at the others and said, “Sorry, but I-I have to go inside.”

Kyle tried to physically stop me, but I managed to get past him. I was a few feet inside before I heard footsteps behind me; I didn’t need to turn around to know they’d followed me inside. The small passage wasn’t interesting; like what I’d seen in the caverns I’d toured as a kid.

Melissa fought her claustrophobia as we continued thru the passage; much to everyone’s relief it soon opened into a small cavern. Turning on my flashlight for more light, I gasped when I saw a piece of machinery in the far corner. “What is that?”

While Liz stayed back to help Melissa with her claustrophobia, Kyle and I walked over to exam the machine. It consisted of a console connected to a moderately sized pad on the floor. Kyle examined the console while I looked at the pad. I could see three poles reaching upward from the back, each with a bump on the top pointing towards the pad’s center.

I was looking up at something attached to ceiling when I heard Kyle call out, “Whatever this is, I’m pretty sure it’s not man-made.”

Walking over with Melissa, Liz said, “What do you mean? This was made naturally?”

Rolling his eyes, Kyle said, “No, I mean I think this was made by aliens.”

Coming around behind Kyle, I saw strange markings that looked nothing like anything I’d ever seen before. Looking closer for a moment, I said, “If that’s that case; then it’s probably been here for a while.” Pausing, I said, “There’s an awful lot of plant growth in here for it to be recent.”

With Melissa and Liz joining Kyle as he examined the console, I decided to focus on the nearby platform. I can’t explain it, but I felt as though it was calling to me. I noticed a faint humming noise the moment I stepped onto the pad; a noise which stopped the instant I stepped off.

With the others focused on the console, and nothing bad happening so far, I decided to step up onto the platform. The humming noise got louder, until the others could hear it as well. I started to get a little nervous; but before I could step off, I felt something pierce my body.

No longer aware of the others, I looked down at some something sticking out of the right side of my stomach. Seeing blood gushing from the wound, I collapsed to the floor of the pad taking with me whatever was impaling me. Suddenly a bright light filled the room; when it cleared, I discovered that I was no longer in the cave – instead I was in a brightly lit room.

I lost consciousness soon after that; only waking up when I heard voices nearby. Slowly opening my eyes, I looked up and saw an alien figure looking down at me.
111.title: Go Inside

1111.content:
It was a struggle for me to remain calm; after all, it’s not every day you see an alien look down at you. The alien, whose gender I couldn’t easily figure out, was about 6ft tall with blue skin, and only 4 fingers on each hand.

Cautiously approaching me, the alien said, “You needn’t worry, Terran; you’re safe here.”

Judging from the deep voice, I quickly figured the alien was male. Looking at him, in disbelief, I said, “Y-You speak English?”

“That’s correct; my species has been observing yours for almost 2000 years.” Gesturing to himself, he said, “My name is Qu’ris D’Pkeni; I’m a Healer at this Medical Facility.”

“A healer?”

“I’m what you’d consider to be a doctor.” Looking at me, he said, “What’s your name?”

Still apprehensive, I said, “My name’s Olivia Sheppard.”

Smiling, Qu’ris said, “What’s the last thing you remember, Olivia Sheppard?”

My memory was a little fuzzy, but I was able to piece together a little of what happened. “My friends and I found this weird device in a cave; I stepped on the platform and suddenly there’s something sticking out of my stomach.”

Nodding, Qu’ris said, “You had a two-foot-long piece of metal sticking out of your lower abdomen.” Checking some information on a tablet, he said, “We were able to remove it, but I’m afraid we weren’t able to save one of your kidneys.”

Pulling up the hospital gown I was wearing, expecting to see surgical scars, I was surprised to see no sign of my injuries. Pulling the gown back down, I said, “I suppose I should thank you for saving my life; but what happens now?”

“Your injuries caused you to lose a lot of blood; we’re searching for someone with compatible blood to be a donor.” Taking a breath, Qu’ris said, “But I suspect that wasn’t what you were referring too.”

Nodding, I said, “When will I be able to return to Earth? Is that even possible?”

“That’s not for me to decide, I’m afraid; but don’t worry, I’ll do what I can.” Smiling, Qu’ris said, “In the meantime, I’ll have a mercy...a nurse bring you some food.”

“Thank you.”
1111.title: Regaining Consciousness

11111.content:
It’s been a few minutes since Qu'ris left, giving me a chance to process what was happening. It wasn’t everyday you find a device that sends you to an alien planet; it would certainly make for a good story to tell. If I ever get back to Earth, that is.

I was still a feeling weak, otherwise I would have gone to the window. I was lying in bed when the door opened. I looked up to see another alien enter the room.

The breasts on the alien's chest told me this one was female; the only other difference from Qu'ris was the shape of her head – somewhat reminiscent of those aliens from the Mass Effect games Eric loved to play.

Approaching me with a small tray, containing a glass with a grayish liquid and covered plate, the alien said, “My name’s Mercy D’Sora, you must be Olivia.”

“I must be; unless there’s another human in this hospital.”

“I’m afraid not; in fact you’re the first Terran many of us has ever seen in person.” Placing the tray on a table, D’Sora said, “Healer D’Pkeni thought you might be hungry; so I gathered the closest things to Terran food that I could find.”

Lifting the tray, I saw what I figured was their version of a sandwich; this one had an orange paste inside it. Taking a tentative bite, I discovered it didn’t taste all that bad. “This isn’t half bad, what’s it called?”

“It’s called no'kata; similar to what you would call tuna.” Gesturing to the glass, D’Sora said, “This is called nestha juice. I know this may look unappealing; but I assure you it tastes delicious.”

Taking a sip of the juice, I realize it tasted quite similar to mangos. Quickly taking another sip, I said, “Thanks for the food, D’Sora.”

“You’re quite welcome, Olivia.” Smiling, she said, “I have more rounds to make, but is there anything you need help with?”

“You could point me in the direction of the restroom; other than that, I’d love to know how long I’ve been here.”

“The bathroom is through that door.” Pointing to a nearby door, D’Sora said, “As for your question; you’ve been here approximately 3 days. The Healer’s thought it best to keep you in an induced coma for a few days.”

I was shocked to learn how much time had passed, but not as much as I’d expected. Looking at D’Sora, I said, “Thanks for your help.”

“Of course.” Pointing to a tablet on the table next to me, she said, “That tablet can let you access our planet’s entire cultural database; if you feel like learning about our culture.”

I would have preferred to watch Netflix or something, but I guess it was better than nothing. Grabbing the tablet, I said, “Thanks."
11111.title: My First Meal

111111.content:
I spent the next few hours using the tablet to immerse myself in the history and culture of this new world. Among the things I learned; this species called themselves Maltorians and their planet was called Maltor. I also learned it was located about 500 light-years from Earth.

Scrolling through the information, I found myself focusing on their religion. These people were deeply religious; their deity was a goddess named Chrestus.

After reading about their religion for a while, I finally felt the need to use the bathroom. With my legs still weakened, I knew I had to be careful. D’Sora must’ve been psychic; because she walked into the room just as I was stepping onto the floor. Rushing over to me, she said, “What are you doing, Olivia?”

“I have to use the bathroom.”

“Well let me help you; you might hurt yourself otherwise.”

It took a few moments to the get to the bathroom, even with the extra help. I sat on the toilet while D’Sora went to grab my empty meal tray. I had barely sat down on the toilet when I heard voices in the other room; one of them belonged to Qu’ris, the other was an unfamiliar girl’s voice.

“Where is she, father?”

“I’m not sure, D’ana.” Knocking on the bathroom door, Qu’ris said, “Olivia, are you in there?”

“Yeah, I’ll be right out.”

It was another couple of minutes before I stepped out of the bathroom, finding Qu’ris standing next to a girl about my age. She was wearing what passed for shorts, plus a short sleeve shirt with a vest.

Gesturing to me, Qu’ris said, “D’ana, this is Olivia Sheppard of Earth; Olivia, this is my daughter, D’ana.”

D’ana and I looked at each other for a few seconds, sizing each other up. She looked about my height and build; I suspect she was considered attractive by Maltorian standards. Stepping forward, her hand out she said, “It’s nice to meet you, Olivia.”

To the surprise of both, I took her hand in mine and said, “May you thrive in the love of Chrestus.”

Taken aback by my remarks, D’ana said, “T-Thank you.”

Looking at me, Qu’ris said, “I see you’ve been reading up on our culture.”

“I hope that’s alright; I’m fascinated about new cultures.”

“It’s fine with me; after all there’s a chance it could become your culture in time.”

I knew what he was referring to, but it wasn’t something I wanted to dwell on. Looking at them both, I said, “What brings you guys here?”

Gesturing towards D’ana, Qu’ris said, “As it turns out, D’ana is a compatible donor for a blood transfusion.” Smiling, he said, “With your blessing, I’d like to set up the transfusion.”

“Sure, go right ahead.” Pausing, I said, “What kind of risks are there; I mean you’ll be pumping alien blood into my system.”

“There will be some minor side effects, for sure, but I’m sure we can manage them.” Turning to the door, he said, “If you’ll both follow me.”
111111.title: Reading the Cultural Database

1111111.content:
Qu’ris set D’ana and me up in a private room down the hall; situating us both on opposite ends of an arch-shaped table, facing each other.

After inserting the needle into his daughter’s arm, Qu’ris came over to do the same with me. “Now the skin around the injection site will turn blue; but that’ll fade once the transfusion is finished.”

Wincing as he put the needle in my arm, I said, “So what should we do to pass the time?”

“That’s entirely up to you two; the transfusion should take about an hour.” Looking at the two of us, he said, “But if I were in your position, I’d like to get to know the person whose blood is being pumped into my arm.”

Qu’ris left after starting the machine, leaving the two of us alone in silence. I watched for a moment as my skin started to turn blue. After a few minutes, I decided to take Qu’ris up on his suggestion. Looking towards D’ana, I said, “So D’ana, why don’t you tell me a little about yourself.”

“There’s really not much to tell; we may have grown up on different worlds, but I suspect our lives aren’t all that dissimilar.”
Pausing, she said, “I spend most of my free time with my best friend and my boyfriend; when I’m not with them, I’m with my younger brother.”

Chuckling, I said, “You weren’t kidding, our lives really are similar; I’m in this situation because I went hiking with my friends.”

Her arm must’ve been sore, because I watched D’ana rubbing it as she said, “Do you have any siblings or a boyfriend?”

“I have a younger brother named Eric, he’s 15; and a boyfriend named Kyle.” Frowning, I said, “I had a twin sister, but she died when we were still babies.”

“I’m sorry to hear that; especially since you likely don’t even remember her.”

“I’m saddened to say you’re right; but thankfully I have photos of the two of us together.” Smiling, I said, “Her name was Deanna; and I like to think we would’ve been close.”

With a kind smile, D’ana said, “I’m sure you would have; I’ve only just met you, but you seem like a kind person.”

“I know we just met, so forgive me if this is out of place; but I’d love to meet your brother and friends.”

Grinning ear to ear, D’ana said, “Sure, you can meet them; as long as you don’t mind answering endless questions about Earth.”

Chuckling, I said, “I’m already learning about your world; I suppose it’s only fair that you learn about mine.”
1111111.title: The Blood Transfusion

11111111.content:
There was no medical reason for D’ana to stay once the transfusion was done; but that didn’t stop her from begging Qu’ris to let her spend the night. He agreed so long as I was okay with it; and I was. I’d come to like D’ana in the short time we’d known each other; we weren’t all different, despite being from two different planets.

So, while the hospital staff brought another bed into my room, D’ana went back to her place to grab some things. Once she returned, she climbed onto the foot of my bed and said, “So what should we do now?”

“I don’t know what it’s like here; but on Earth, in situations like this, girls our age usually discuss boys they like.” Looking at her, I said, “Since the two of us already have boyfriends, perhaps we could talk about them?”

Jumping off the bed for a moment, D’ana retrieved a small device about the size of my cellphone. Doing something on it, she handed it to me and said, “That’s me and boyfriend To’ran at the Festival of Healing last month.”

Looking at the photo, I could see D’ana and To’ran standing underneath a tree covered in decorations. The first thing that caught my attention, apart from the decorations, was the fact that he was kind of cute – his skin being a slightly darker shade of blue then D’ana. Blushing ever so slightly as I handed the device back to her, I said, “I hope this isn’t weird or anything; but he’s kind of cute.”

Chuckling, D’ana said, “Since you think my boyfriend’s cute; I hope you don’t mind if I think yours is as well.”

Grabbing my cellphone from the table, I said, “I suppose it’s only fair.” Scrolling through my photos, I found a good one of Kyle and me at the beach. Handing the phone to D’ana, I said, “This is Kyle and me at the beach a few weeks back.”

Looking at the photo, D’ana said, “Wow; I was originally going to say he was cute to mess with you, but he really is cute.” Handing the phone back to me, D’ana said, “Have you two been intimate yet?”

I was taken aback by that question, especially since I’d only just met D’ana. Looking at her, I said, “No, not yet; although Kyle’s been on my case about it for a few months. How about you and To’ran?”

“Oh yes; we’ve been intimate many times.” Smiling, D’ana said, “In fact; to put it in terms you’re familiar with, I lost my virginity to him when I was 13.”

Stunned, I said, “You lost your virginity at 13? Did your parents ever find out?”

“Of course; in fact, losing it to To’ran was their idea.” Seeing the look on my face, D’ana said, “I guess I should give you an explanation. When female Maltorians reach the age of 13 their body enters a second, more aggressive, stage of puberty – called va’lasho. If we don’t have sex within a week, we go temporarily insane.”

“I guess I missed that part when I was searching the database.” Looking at her, I said, “On Earth, having sex that young is heavily frowned upon; it’s led to a lot of teenage pregnancies.”

“Well, we don’t worry about that here; females are implanted with a device that makes it impossible to get pregnant before the age of 18.” Blushing slightly, D’ana said, “I can't to get it removed in a few months; not that I'm in a rush to get pregnant."
11111111.title: In Recovery

111111111.content:
D’ana and I stayed up well into the evening; just talking and hanging out. It amazed me how easily we got along, despite being from two different species.

I told her about life on Earth, while she told me about every day life on Maltor; stuff I wouldn’t have gotten from the cultural database.

I was the first one to wake up the next morning; it seemed my biological clock still worked on another planet. Sitting up in bed, I noticed my felt arm sore around the injection site.

I looked down to see that my skin hadn’t returned to its normal color yet, it was still a pale blue. I’m sure it’s nothing. After making a mental note to ask Qu’ris about it, I got up to use the bathroom.

***********


D’ana was awake by the time I got out of the bathroom. Looking at me, she said, “How’d you sleep last night?”

“Pretty good, actually; I had a few dreams where I was back on Earth.” Sitting on the edge of my bed, I said, “How about you?”

“I’ve never been to Earth; so, I didn’t have any dreams about it.” With a grin, she said, “But I slept fine.”

“Your dad said something about another transfusion this morning; how many of these am I supposed to get?”

“I’m not sure; but father did say you lost a lot of blood.” Pausing, D’ana said, “So I guess it’ll be as many as he thinks is necessary.”

I was silent for a moment, thinking about my future. “What are the chances I’ll be able to return to Earth?”

“Again, I have no idea.” Looking at me, D’ana said, “Nothing like this has ever happened before; worst case you’ll live out your life here.”

Frowning, I said, “If that’s the case, I hope they’d let me send a goodbye message to my family.”

“I can understand why you want to do that; but you may want to reconsider if it comes down to that.”

Curious, I said, “Why?”

“We’re in a conflict with another species, called the Creel. They’re a merciless enemy; the last thing you want to do is reveal Earth’s location to them.”

Starting to get worked up, I said, “Can we talk about something else, please?”

“Sure; perhaps you’d like to meet my brother and my friends after the transfusion is done?”

“Sure, why not; I always like meeting new people.”
111111111.title: The Next Day

1111111111.content:
Qu’ris came by after D’ana and I had breakfast; I was beginning to enjoy Maltorian food.

I decided to inquire about the blue skin as he hooked me up, “The skin around the injection site hasn’t changed back to my normal color yet. Is that something I should be concerned about?”

“I’m sure it’s nothing to be concerned about; but let me know if you experience any other skin discoloration.” Looking at me, he said, “Is there anything else I need to know about?”

The pinky finger on my right arm was pretty stiff; but I figured I must’ve slept on it wrong. Looking at Qu'ris, I said, “Nothing a day at the beach won’t cure.”

Chuckling, Qu’ris said, “My family is taking a trip to one of the more prestigious tropical resorts; I'd be honored if you'd come along, and I'm sure D'ana would enjoy the company."

I could tell from the smile on her face that D'ana enjoyed that idea. Smiling, I said, "Sure, I'd like that."
1111111111.title: Qu'ris Stops By

11111111111.content:
The transfusion was just as uneventful as the first one; D’ana and I passed the time talking about Earth again. It wasn’t until that moment that I realized something. Looking at D’ana, I said, “Hey, you wouldn’t happen to know what time it is on Earth; would you?”

Reaching for her tablet, D’ana said, “Where on Earth?”

“West Virginia.”

Looking at the screen, D’ana said, “It’s around 7:30 in the evening. Why?”

Trying to hide my sadness, I said, “Just curious; found myself wondering what my family was doing right now.” Sighing, I said,
“They’re probably still trying to figure out what happened to me.”

“I have faith that you’ll see them again, Olivia.” Taking my hand in hers, D’ana said, “Chrestus loves to reunite separated families; I’m sure she’s guiding events to do just that.”

I wanted to believe D’ana was correct, but I didn’t want to get my hopes up. “You’re more optimistic than I am.”

The transfusion ended a few minutes later; Qu’ris entered the room carrying something in his hands. It wasn’t until he handed it to me that I realized it was an outfit like what D’ana wore the first time I’d met her. “D’ana thought you might be more comfortable in something that wasn’t a hospital gown.” Pausing, he said, “I used the measurements from your file; but please let me know if the clothes don’t fit.”

D’ana and Qu’ris left to give me some privacy while I changed. Upon closer inspection I realized the outfit was made of something like velvet. I couldn’t find a bra until I realized the shirt had one built into it. It took me a few minutes to get dressed; but once I was finished, I looked in the mirror and realized I looked like someone from Star Trek.

After putting on the vest, I opened the door and walked outside. Doing a quick spin, I said, “How do I look?”

Qu’ris and D’ana looked at me for a second; D’ana soon responded, “I think a lot of boys are going to be interested in you.”

Qu’ris interjected before I could respond, “Speaking of boys; your brother is waiting outside with your friends. They’re all anxious to meet Olivia; so long as she’s up to it.”

As they both turned their attention to me, I said, “I’m okay with it; I was starting to feel cooped up in here anyway.”

“Now Olivia, you’re still getting your strength back; would you like a hover chair or would you rather walk?”

“I think I’d rather walk; I’m sure D’ana can help if I get tired.”
11111111111.title: A Second Transfusion

111111111111.content:
I got my first real look at Maltor as D’ana and I stepped out of the hospital. It reminded me of every futuristic city I’d seen in the movies; like Coruscant, or San Francisco from Star Trek. There were tall buildings everywhere, but that didn’t prevent nature from popping up; there was a modest looking pond a short distance from the hospital, surrounded by trees and a path.

Standing next to me, D’ana said, “Well, what do you think?”

In awe, I said, “This is impressive; I wish my people could build like this.”

“I’m sure they will; given enough time.” Pointing towards the pond, D’ana said, “Come on; I see my brother down by the water.”

While I was focused on the scenery, I noticed a lot of the eyes were on me. Everyone staring at me reminded of my first day wearing braces. Turning to D’ana, in a hushed tone, I said, “How long before everyone stops staring at me?”

D’ana stopped and looked at me, “Most Maltorians never get a chance to see an alien species up close; so, this a once in a lifetime opportunity for most of us.” Pausing, D’ana said, “Give it some time and they’ll eventually move on.”

I wanted to believe D’ana was correct, but deep down I suspected she wasn’t. I saw four Maltorians waiting by the pond; as we got closer, I could tell they were two boys and two girls. Stopping in front of them, D’ana said, “Everyone, I’d like you to meet Olivia Sheppard of Earth.” Turning to me, she said, “Olivia; this is my brother Narek, my boyfriend To’ran, and my friends Asura and Sorain.”

Once the pleasantries were exchanged Asura stepped forward and said, “I’m actually Narek’s best friend, but I also hang out with D’ana.”

The six of us sat in the shade of a nearby tree; I watched as a small bird, with two sets of wings, flew around overhead. Once it flew off, I turned back to the others and said, “D’ana warned me you guys might have questions about Earth.”

The four of them exchanged looks with each other; no one said a word until Narek turned back to me, “We do have questions, but will only ask them if you’re okay with it.”

“Sure, go right ahead; so long as you’re okay with me asking questions of my own.”
111111111111.title: Stepping Outside (Making Friends)

1111111111111.content:
The six of us spent a few hours sitting outside talking; taking turns answering questions. I was amused by the misconceptions some of them had about Earth; To’ran thought it had two moons.

When I asked how there could be misconceptions about Earth if their species had been watching mine for 2000 years, D’ana said, “Do you know everything about every culture on Earth?”

“When you put it that way, I suppose that makes sense.”

Suddenly the silence was shattered by a low, distant rumble. I watched as everyone got to their feet; looking at me, Narek said, “It’d probably be smart to go inside; lightning storms on Maltor can be quite severe compared to those on Earth.”

A bolt of lightning struck the ground probably two miles away; despite that distance it sounded like it was much closer. It was if though Chrestus was proving Narek’s point. Getting to my feet, I said, “So what do you guys want to do now?”

As it turned out everyone, but Narek had things to attend to; D’ana had to attend practice with Asura and Sorain, while To’ran had to work. After saying goodbye to the others, Narek and I watched from under the entrance overhang as they left. Once they were gone, I turned to him and said, “So what sport does D’ana play with Asura and Sorain?”

“It’s called binejuc.” turning to me, he said, “The closest thing you’d be familiar with is basketball; only it’s played in zero-gravity.”

My interest piqued, I said, “Wow; that actually sounds like it’d be fun.”

The whole time we’d hung out under the tree, I couldn’t shake the feeling that Narek was interested in me. Despite my relationship with Kyle, I wasn’t ashamed to admit I found Narek attractive as well. Blushing as he looked at me, Narek said,
“Perhaps I could take you to a game once you’re out of the hospital?”

Okay, perhaps I should nip this in the bud. Nervously rubbing my arm, I said, “Narek, I’m sure you’re a sweet guy; but I’ve got a boyfriend back on Earth.”

Looking at me, his feelings obviously hurt, Narek said, “Oh, I-I see.”

Feeling terrible, I decided to boost his spirts, “Who knows; if it turns out that I’m stuck here, I’d love to go out with you.”

With a smile on his face, Narek said, “I hope this doesn’t come out wrong; but I kind of hope you are stuck here.”

“Who knows; Chrestus works in mysterious ways.” Placing a hand on his shoulder, I said, “Now what do you say we get something to eat?”

“Sure; I’ll show you to the cafeteria.”
1111111111111.title: Continue

11111111111111.content:
Narek and I talked as we rode the lift to the cafeteria on the third floor. He seemed like a genuinely nice guy; if it weren’t for Kyle, I’d be more than willing to see where things went. Stopping outside the cafeteria, I was puzzled to find rows of lockers situated to one side.

I turned to ask Narek what that was all about; only to find that he’d vanished. I was about to call out for him when he stepped out from behind a nearby wall – completely naked. Covering my eyes, I said, “What the hell, Narek? Why are you naked?”

“I suspect you didn’t read that part of the cultural database.” Stepping over to me, he said, “Maltorians eat all their meals naked.”

I don’t know what shocked me more; the revelation itself, or the fact that Narek had failed to tell me about it. “A little heads up would have been nice. Would I be breaking some rule if I didn’t undress myself?”

“That’s totally up to you, Olivia; no one will hold it against you.”

I struggled with my decision for a few minutes; until I eventually made my choice. I stepped out of the changing area a few minutes later; only to find Narek staring at me. Covering myself, I said, “I know it’s part of your culture, but I’d appreciate it if you stopped starring.”

“Sorry.” Looking away, Narek said, “Follow me inside; I’ll help you choose your meal.”
11111111111111.title: A Surprise at Lunch

111111111111111.content:
My lunch with Narek wasn’t as weird as I thought; especially once I got over the fact that everyone in the room was naked. My hesitation, however, led to Narek asking me some questions. I spent longer than I would have expected discussing the concept of nudists on Earth.

Narek escorted me back to my room once lunch was over. Standing outside the door, I said, “I had a wonderful time with you, Narek; even with the unexpected nudity.”

“Same here, Olivia; perhaps we could do it again sometime?”

I could see where Narek was going with this; and part of me wanted to set the record straight. But a small, but growing, part of me didn’t want to risk alienating one of the few friends I’d made here. Smiling, I said, “Let’s see where the fates take us, Narek; alright?”

“Sure.” Smiling, he said, “I’ll see you later.”

*****************************


I decided to read some more of the cultural database; that way I had no more surprises like the nudity. I was lying in bed, reading the tablet, when Qu’ris walked into the room. The look on his face told me that he had something important to tell me. Putting the tablet down, I said, “What’s up?”

“I’ve spoken with the hospital administrator; she’s agreed to let me discharge you from the facility.” Pausing, Qu’ris said, “You’ll still have to return for a few more transfusions, but you won’t have to spend the night here.”

“That’s great; I’ll be honest, I’m not a real fan of hospitals.” Feeling a sense of relief, I said, “So does that mean I’m staying with you?”

“I’m sure both D’ana and yourself would like that very much; but sadly that’s not the case.” Sitting on the edge of my bed, Qu’ris said, “Our home isn’t large enough for you to move in; but don’t worry, I’ve made arrangements that I believe you’ll find satisfactory.”

Trying my best to hide my disappointment, I said, “Oh?”

“Do you remember my son’s friend, Asura?” looking at me, Qu’ris said, “Her family has agreed to let you stay with them.”

During the brief time that I knew her, I got the sense that Asura was a nice person. As much as I wanted to stay with D’ana, she wasn’t a bad alternative. “Okay, so when do I get to leave?”

“You’ll have to spend one last night here; so, you’ll leave with them tomorrow.” Smiling, Qu’ris said, “Now you’ve had an exciting day; why don’t you get some rest.”
111111111111111.title: Returning From Lunch

1111111111111111.content:
I wasn’t sure how long I’d slept; but I could still see a little sunlight pouring through the window and could hear birds chirping outside. Sitting up in bed, I stopped when I realized I was no longer in the hospital; I was back in my bedroom on Earth. What the hell? Looking around, I thought, was all that a dream?

Climbing out of bed, I ran downstairs to find my family. Going into the kitchen, I saw them all sitting at the kitchen table. Looking at me, Mom said, “Olivia, sweetie, what’s wrong?”

“I-I don’t know; I just had the weirdest dream.” Sitting at the kitchen table, I said, “I dreamt that I got injured on my hike; only to wake up on this alien world.”

I continued describing the dream; once I was finished, Mom said, “That does sound like an interesting dream; let’s just hope it’s not a prophetic one.”

“Huh?”

“Sweetie; you haven’t gone on the hike yet.” Pointing to the calendar, Mom said, “You leave in a few hours.”

I looked at the calendar and realized that Mom was right; today was the day I was leaving on the hike. “Huh, guess I was just nervous about the hike.” Getting up from the table, I said, “I should probably finish my packing.”

I was in the middle of packing, about 20 minutes later, when Mom knocked on my door. Walking inside, she said, “I hope you have enough warm clothes, sweetie; I just wish we could find a hat that fits you properly.”

Confused once again, I said, “What are you talking about?”

Walking up behind me, Mom said, “Your unique head shape makes it difficult to find hats that fit; don’t you remember?”

I got a good look at my reflection as Mom turned me towards the mirror; and it wasn’t what I’d expected at all. Instead of my usual human self, I saw a Maltorian looking back at me – complete with the blue skin and the oddly shaped skull of a Maltorian female. “What the…”

I awoke with a start; gasping as I shot up in bed. I looked around, as I calmed down, to find that I was still in the Maltorian hospital. Sitting back in the bed, I wondered why I had a dream where I was a Maltorian. “That was weird.”

No longer in the mood to nap, I reached for the tablet once more; only to find that the pinky finger on my right hand wasn't moving like the others. "What's going on here?"
1111111111111111.title: Qu'ris Pays a Visit

11111111111111111.content:
After trying unsuccessfully to get my pinky to uncurl, I managed to do it with my other hand – only to watch as it curled up on its own. I was on the verge of panicking when I heard a knock on the door; followed by, “Olivia, it’s Maiya; can I come in?”

Not recognizing the name, I hopped out of bed and went to the door. Opening it a crack, I saw D’Sora standing just outside. Opening the door wider, I said, “Is Maiya your first name?”

Maiya looked confused for a moment, until she realized what was going on. Smacking the side of her head, she said, “Goddess; I never told you my first name, did I?”

I found myself briefly hung up on the goddess part, until I realized their deity was female. “No, you didn’t; but it’s a lovely name.” stepping to the side, I said, “What brings you here?”

"I was on my way home; but I thought I'm check on you before I did.”

For a moment I considered not telling her about my hand, thinking it was nothing to be concerned about. Then a voice in my head told me that I should. Raising my hand to show her, I said, “I can’t seem to uncurl my pinky; when I do, it just curls back up again.”

Maiya did the same thing I did, uncurling my finger with her hand only to see it curl back up again. Growing concerned, she said, “I’ve never heard of anything like this happening before; we should probably tell Healer D’Pkeni.”

***************************


Qu’ris arrived within minutes; it seemed that my wellbeing was of major concern to him. Once he saw what was going on, he decided to run every test he could think of. Maiya and I were talking while we anxiously awaited the results.

“So Maiya, are you seeing anyone right now?”

“Sadly no; ever since our parents were killed, I’ve been too busy raising Sorain to have a love life.” Frowning, she said, “Next month will be 8 years since they died.”

“If you don’t mind my asking; how did they die?”

“They were aboard a ship heading to a recently established colony.” Pausing, she said, “The ship was attacked by the Creel.”

Hearing that there was another alien race out there piqued my interest, “Who are the Creel?”

“They’re a species from a neighboring system; we’ve been fighting them for close almost two centuries.” Taking a breath, Maiya said, “Pray you never come in contact with them; because you’d wish you hadn’t.”

“Why?”

“They transform anyone they capture into mindless drones; whose sole purpose is laying down their lives for the Creel.”

All the color drained from my face as I heard that; especially once I realized that had been the fate of Maiya and Sorain’s parents. Before I could even begin to process everything, Qu’ris entered the room with a mournful look on his face. “The results are in, Olivia; and I’m afraid they’re not good.”

“Wh-What do you mean?”

“It seems that the blood you’re receiving from D’ana is having an unforeseen side effect.” Taking a deep breath, Qu’ris said, “You’re becoming Maltorian.”
11111111111111111.title: D'Sora Comes to Visit

111111111111111111.content:
I couldn’t figure out who was more shocked by that shocking twist; Qu’ris, Maiya or myself. Focusing my attention on Qu’ris, I said, “Co-Could you say that again?”

Stepping closer, a hint of sadness in his voice, Qu’ris said, “The tests show that your body is having an unforeseen reaction to the blood transfusion.” Looking at me, he said, “The Maltorian DNA in the blood is overwriting your Terran DNA.”

I started shaking my head in disbelief, “T-That’s not possible.” Getting angry, I said, “You told me there’d be no side effects! You promised me!”

“No, I told you there’d be minor side effects that we’d handle as they came up.” Looking at me, Qu’ris said, “I never anticipated this sort of reaction. I never would have gone through with this if I thought something like this would happen.”

Struggling to calm down, my thoughts turned to D’ana, “If I’m turning into a Maltorian, does that mean D’ana is turning into a human?”

“No, D’ana isn’t experiencing any side effects from the transfusion.”

Holding me close, Maiya said, “How long before Olivia is fully Maltorian, Healer?”

“Nothing like this has happened before, so I have no way of knowing for sure.” Covering his mouth briefly, Qu’ris said, “If I had to venture a guess, I’d say that Olivia will be almost entirely Maltorian by the end of the week.”

I sat silently on the bed while I processed everything. After a moment, I looked at Maiya and Qu’ris, “If you don’t mind, I’d like to be alone for a while.”

They both nodded silently as they turned to leave. Before closing the door, Qu’ris said, “I’m truly sorry, Olivia; I hope you know I never wanted this to happen.”

**************************


I lay on the bed silently for a long time; struggling to process everything I’d learned. Even if I were lucky enough to return to Earth, I’d be an alien on my own planet. Chances were good that my friends and family wouldn’t even recognize me.

I was about to get out of bed to use the bathroom when I heard a knock at the door. Not in the mood for visitors, I said, “Please go away.”

There was silence for a moment, until the door slowly opened to reveal D’ana with a sad look on her face. Stepping inside, she said, “My Dad told me what happened; I thought I’d come to see how you were doing.”

Standing just outside the bathroom, I said, “I’m afraid I won’t be good company; but you can stay if you want.”
111111111111111111.title: Qu'ris Explains

1111111111111111111.content:
I wound up spending almost half an hour in the bathroom; most of it spent just staring at my reflection in the mirror. After hearing the news from Qu’ris, I began to wonder how much longer I’d have my human face.

Walking out of the bathroom, I sat D’ana sitting on the edge of my bed. Looking at me, she said, “How are you feeling?”

“I’m turning into an alien, D’ana.” Letting my anger briefly boil over, I said, “How do you think I feel?”

“I can’t say, since I was born a member of the species you’re turning into.” Looking at me as I sat on the bed, D’ana said, “But if I were to suddenly start turning into a Terran, I’d probably act the same way you are.”

“Even if they let me return to Earth; chances are good that I’ll stop being human long before then.” Tearing up, I said, “My parents, my brother, my friends: they won’t even recognize me anymore.”

“I know you probably don’t want to hear this right now; but would it be so bad if you didn’t return to Earth?” grasping my hand in hers, D’ana said, “Assuming you get permission; your loved ones will have probably moved on by that point. Seeing how much you’ve changed might not sit too well with them.”

The more I listened to D’ana, the more I realized she might be right. Looking at her, I said, “Let’s say I don’t go back; what will happen to me then?”

“You’d live out your life here on Maltor; falling in love and raising a family.”

Lying back in bed, I said, “You make it all sound so nice; but deep down I doubt I’d ever stop thinking about my family.”

Lying next to me, D’ana said, “I’m sure, whatever happens, Chrestus will look out for them.”
1111111111111111111.title: Alone with D'ana

11111111111111111111.content:
D’ana and I stayed up in well into the evening, talking and enjoying each other's company. Eventually we both got tired and fell asleep, only for me to wake up a few hours later. Sitting up in bed, careful not the wake D’ana, I wondered what could’ve woken me up. I got my answer when I suddenly realized my head felt like it was being compressed in a vice.

The pain I was experiencing was worse then even the most painful migraine I’d ever experience. Desperate for answers, I climbed out of bed and ran into the bathroom. Looking at my reflection in the mirror, I didn’t see anything unusual – at least until I turned my head to the side. What the hell? While it was still in the preliminary stages, my skull was starting to take on a shape like D’ana’s.

Hoping to get a closer look, I went to lift my hair with one hand. I stopped when I saw that my little finger had been reduced to a nub. Great, thinking as I looked in the mirror, the hits just keep on coming.

I was about to examine my skull more closely when there was a knock on the door. Before I had a chance to say anything, D’ana walked in. Stopping in her tracks when she saw my skull, “Wow, my dad wasn’t kidding when he said you were turning into a Maltorian.”

“Trust me, I wish he was kidding.” Sighing as I lowered my hair, I said, “He said I’d be fully Maltorian by the end of the week; but something tells me that estimate was a bit off.”

Moving next to me, D’ana took my hand in hers and said, “I’ll be with you every step of the way, Olivia.”

“Thanks, D’ana.”
11111111111111111111.title: During the Night

111111111111111111111.content:
It was difficult for me to get any sleep for the rest of the night; the pain was unbearable as my head continued to change shape. I think I only managed to get a couple of hours of sleep before the sun appeared in the window.

Sitting up in bed, the first thing I noticed was a pile of hair on my pillow. That wasn’t all that surprising, since Maltorians were completely bald. It just served as a reminder that I would soon cease to be human.

Getting out of bed, I walked into the bathroom to look at the extent of the changes. I quickly noticed that my once shoulder-length hair had been reduced to nothing more than a buzz cut. I also noticed that my skull now noticeably resembled that of a Maltorian female – although not as developed as D’ana’s.

Speaking of D’ana, I didn’t know she was standing behind me until I heard her say, “Looks like you’re well on your way to becoming Maltorian.”

“It sure looks that way, doesn’t it?” Turning to look at her, gesturing to my skull, I said, “What is the point of this, anyway? Why don’t males go through this change?”

“It’s an effect of puberty; just like breast development.” Growing concerned, D’ana said, “I just had a thought; if you’re this far along already, it won’t be long before you undergo va’lasho.”

After learning about va’lasho from D’ana, I wasn’t exactly thrilled by the prospect of going through it myself. “I guess I have to find a guy to have sex with, huh?”

With a nod, D’ana said, “That would probably be a smart idea.”

There was a knock on the door a few minutes later, followed by Qu’ris walking into the room. I don’t know what he came in to tell me; but he was certainly stunned when he saw what happened to me. “Well, there’s no doubt you’re becoming Maltorian.” Looking at me, he said, “How are you feeling, Olivia?”

“My head feels like it’s in a vice; can you give me something for the pain?”

“Of course; I’ll see what I can do.” Taking a break, Qu’ris said, “Before I forget; Asura and her family are here to take you home. Are you ready?”

I started to feel uncertain about my future. Looking at Qu’ris, with a shrug, I said, “I guess so.”
111111111111111111111.title: The Changes Continue

1111111111111111111111.content:
After changing into a fresh out, and getting a clean bill of health from Qu’ris, I followed him and D’ana out of the room. There were a few stares as we made our way to the lift; only now I was used to them. I may resemble a Maltorian-Human hybrid now, but I knew it would only be a matter of days before I was completely Maltorian.

Entering the lift, I turned to Qu’ris with a question, “D’ana already told me about va’lasho, and what to expect from that.” Taking a breath, I said, “What other changes can I expect to go through before all is said and done?”

Pressing the button for the bottom floor, Qu’ris said, “There aren’t many anatomical differences between our two species; but there are a few.” Turning to me, he continued, “Our hearts are twice the size of a Terrans, and we only have one of what you call a kidney.”

My face turning pale, I said, “I have a feeling I’m going to be for some pain when that change occurs.”

“I’m sure you will, but we can help you with that.” Looking at me as the lift reached the bottom, and the door opened, “In the meantime, you should focus on adjusting to your life here on Maltor.”

Stepping out of the lift, I said, “That reminds me; have you heard anything about my returning to Earth?”

“I’m afraid not; the Council has yet to respond to my request for a meeting.” Placing a sympathetic hand on my shoulder, Qu’ris said, “Don’t worry; I’ll tell you the minute I hear anything.”

With that, the three of us exited the building. It didn’t take long to spot Asura standing nearby with two older Maltorians. As I approached her, Asura looked at me and said, “I guess D’ana wasn’t kidding when she said you were becoming Maltorian.”
Embracing me, she said, “How are you doing?”

“Pretty good, I guess.” Breaking the hug, I looked at her and said, “It’s not every day that you start turning into another species.”

Gesturing to the adults behind her, Asura said, “These are my parents; Jervix and Malura Telfani.”

After exchanging pleasantries, Jervix said, “I know this is all new to you, Olivia; but I promise that my wife and I will treat you as if you were our biological daughter.”

“Thanks; I appreciate you agreeing to take me in.”

“Think nothing of it, Olivia.” Stepping closer, Malura said, “Once she heard you needed a place to stay, Asura practically begged us to take you in.”

I looked at Asura with a raised eyebrow; her response was, “I always wanted to have a sister.”

At that point, Qu’ris walked over and handed a satchel to Jervix, “This contains all of Olivia’s personal effects; and some pain killers.” Glancing at me, he said, “I can’t be sure; but I suspect that she’ll start experiencing the more painful aspects of her transformation within a day or so. Please contact me the moment it starts.”

“Or course, Qu’ris.” Satchel in hand, Jervix said, “We’ll take good care of her.”

After saying goodbye to Qu’ris and D’ana, I followed Asura and her parents as they left the area. We eventually reached what I figured was a parking garage; filled with vehicles resembling the cars from Minority Report. Approaching one of them, Jervix turned to me and said, “You must be hungry, Olivia; how about we get some lunch before heading home?”

“Sure; I could definitely go for something that isn’t hospital food.”
1111111111111111111111.title: Leaving the Hospital

11111111111111111111111.content:
As we left the Medical Center, I was impressed by the fact that we were in a self-driving car. Looking at Jervix as he and Malura spun around to face the back, I said, “This is so cool; I’ve never been in a self-driving car before.”

Turning to me, Asura said, “They’re not available on Earth?”

“Not really; there are a few companies testing them but they’re not readily available yet.” Looking at Jervix, I said, “So how does it work; do you just tell it where to go?”

“That’s right; each runabout is programmed to a specific family.” Smiling, Jervix said, “I’ll have to remember to program your voice into the computer.”

The conversation continued as the runabout entered what I could only imagine was a Maltorian highway. After journeying down that for a few minutes, we soon found ourselves in line with other runabouts. Ever curious, I said, “Where are we?”

“We’re at what you’d consider to be a drive thru.” Turning to me, Malura said, “I know your experience with Maltorian food is limited, but is there anything you’d prefer to have?”

Thinking back to my first meal at the Medical Center, I looked at Malura and said, “I could go for anything with no’kata in it.”

“Excellent; so that’ll be two no’kata wraps for the girls, and I’ll have a pan’gu sandwich.” Turning to her husband, Malura said, “What about you, Jervix?”

Jervix settled on a no’kata wrap as well. After receiving our order, he programmed home in as the destination, and we were off once again. As we journeyed down the highway, eating our food, I found myself thinking back to all the times I’d done this same thing with my family. Here I was on an alien planet, lightyears from Earth, and I couldn’t help but feel at home. You know, maybe being stuck here wouldn’t be so bad.
11111111111111111111111.title: Getting Lunch

111111111111111111111111.content:
After another 20 minutes of travel, we arrived at our destination: a modest two-story home on a quiet street. The first thought that came to mind as the runabout came to a stop was how similar the area was to neighborhoods on Earth. Even the homes look like they’d fit it on Earth.

The four of us got out of the runabout and went inside just as it started to rain. Once we were inside, Malura turned to Asura and me, “Asura, why don’t you show Olivia around the house.”

“Okay.” Turning to me, Asura said, “Come on, we’ll start the tour upstairs.”

Still unsure about everything, I followed after Asura as she went upstairs. We went down a short hallway before stopping outside of one of the rooms. Peeking inside, I saw two beds built into the wall. Turning to Asura, I said, “I’m guessing this is our bedroom?”

Walking past me on her way inside, Asura said, “That’s right; if you want, you can have the top bunk.”

With Asura watching from the doorway I looked around the rest of the room, paying particular attention to the shelf full of trophies. The room reminded me a lot of my own room on Earth; there were even posters from what I figured were Maltorian bands. I made a note to inquire about Maltorian music before turning back to Asura, “This reminds of my room on Earth; even if it’s a little spartan.”

“It may seem that way, but I assure you it’s not.” Reaching for a tablet on the wall, Asura pressed a button and a desk appeared out of nowhere, “We use something called programable matter to create whatever furniture or entertainment device me might need.”

“Wow, that’s pretty cool.”

After that, Asura continued giving me a tour of the house; showing me the sonic shower in the bathroom and the matter reclaimer they used in place of a washer & dryer. That last part didn't make sense until Asura explained how Maltorian clothes were made from reclaimed material. Looking at her as we stepped out of the room, I said, "I suppose that explains why you didn't have a closet in your room."

"Oh I do; it's just hidden behind a wall panel. I keep important clothes in there; like my binejuc suit, or the garment I wear for religious services." looking at me, she said, "There's plenty of room for you to put things in there too, if you'd like."

The rain had stopped by that point, so Asura thought it would be nice to sit outside for a while. Malura and Jervix were nowhere to be seen. I was about to inquire as to where they were when my stomach started growling. “I’m getting kind of thirsty; you got anything to drink?”

Indicating that I should follow her, Asura said, “You bet.”

Asura opened the door on what I quickly figured was a refrigerator, or the Maltorian equivalent of one. I watched her take out a pitcher of neshta juice and pour it into two glasses. Taking one of them, I took a sip and said, “I must say; Maltorian food certainly tastes better than it looks.”

Walking towards the back door, Asura said, “I should probably warn you; you’ll have trouble digesting human food once your new anatomy asserts itself.”

As I followed Asura outside I thought to myself, the hits just keep on coming.

Asura and I sat in chairs on the back porch watching as the clouds disappeared. Only once they were all gone did I discover something I hadn’t seen before – there were two stars in the sky. “I’ve been here for three days; why didn’t I noticed there were two stars?”

“Maltor is one of 5 planets in a binary system; Uzras is the closest star, while Ekela is the furthest.” Smiling, Asura said, “They eclipse each other every 15 days; you just happened to go outside when that was happening.”

Looking at them both, shielding my eyes, I said, “I’m sure those eclipses make for a fantastic viewing experience.”

“They do; but it gets evening better.” Smiling, Asura said, “Once a year our orbit brings us in line with them both, which results in some pretty intense auroras.”
111111111111111111111111.title: Reaching our Destination

1111111111111111111111111.content:
Asura and I sat outside for an hour just watching the scenery and talking. We had just finished discussing my life on Earth; when I realized I didn’t know a whole lot about her. “I can’t help but feel like I’ve been hogging the spotlight here; telling you all about my life without even asking about yours.” Turning to face her, I said, “You know practically everything about me; while all I know about you is that Narek’s your best friend and you play a binejuc with D’ana.”

“There’s really not a lot to tell you.” Looking straight ahead, she said, “I’m so busy with school and binejuc, I don’t even have time for a relationship.”

“That’s too bad; because I’m sure there are all sorts of guys out there who’d be interested.”

“I’m sure there are but I wouldn’t be interested in them.” Turning to me, Asura said, “I’m what Terrans would consider to be a lesbian.”

I was stunned; that possibility never occurred to me. “Wow; I had no idea.” Pausing, I said, “Although now that I think about it; that would explain some of the looks you were giving me at the lake yesterday.”

“I was hoping you wouldn’t notice that.” Asura blushed, “Yes; I’ll admit I found you attractive, but then I realized you were straight. That won’t make things weird, will it?”

“Of course not; one of my best friends is a lesbian.” With a smile, I said, “I actually think you’d like her; you’re both a lot alike.”

“Who knows; I might get a chance someday.”
1111111111111111111111111.title: Bonding with Asura

11111111111111111111111111.content:
Asura and I stayed outside until the suns set; eventually going back upstairs to our bedroom. On the way, I felt the urge to use the bathroom. After relieving myself, I was about to wash my hands when I saw that both my little fingers had disappeared. Huh, looking at my hands, I didn’t even feel a thing.

I was marveling at my four-fingered hands when Asura entered the bathroom, “My mom thought you’d like to know dinner is almost ready.” Seeing the look on my face, she said, “Are you okay?”

Showing her my hands, I said, “I’m now one step closer to being a Maltorian.” Leaning against the counter, I said, “I haven’t looked at my feet since this morning; but I can only imagine they’re not far behind.”

“It probably doesn’t feel that way now; but I promise you’ll be okay.” Embracing me, Asura said, “You’ve only been here a few days; but you’ve already built up quite a support system.”

I felt my anxiety start to disappear as the two of us hugged; Asura was right, I did have a support system to help me through this.
“Thanks for that, Asura.” Breaking away from the hug, I said, “Now you said something about dinner being ready?”

“I said it was almost ready; but that was a few minutes ago.” Opening the door, Asura said, “Let’s get undressed and head downstairs.”

Compared to my lunch with Narek, I found that I wasn’t uncomfortable with the idea of being naked around Asura. Maybe it was the fact that we were both girls, I don’t know. Throwing our clothes in our bedroom, the two of us walked downstairs just as Malura was placing a pot on the table – one that resembled those oriental tables I’d seen on TV.

After placing the pot, Malura turned to face Asura and me. Taking notice of my hands, she said, “Ah, I see your hands have changed. You should be one of us before too long.”

I simply smiled as I took my seat next to Asura; I wasn’t thrilled about what was happening, and Malura’s positivity wasn’t helping. Jervix entered the room a moment later holding a gold statuette in his hands – I could only assume it was of Chrestus. Looking at me as he placed the statuette on the table, Jervix said, “It’s my understanding that certain Terran cultures pray to a deity before every meal; we do the same here on Maltor.”

I had no idea what to do next, so I copied everything that Asura did. After saying our prayers to Chrestus, Malura removed the lid from the pot. I peeked inside and saw an odd looking tentacled-creature. I also took note of the fact that it was still alive. “Malura; I don’t mean to be rude, but I don’t think you cooked this enough.”

Malura sliced off a tentacle and placed it on my plate, “It’s best to eat penghou when it’s alive; or else it’s decaying body creates a deadly neurotoxin.”

Part of me considered declining the food until I realized that would be rude. I also remembered what Asura had said about only being able to eat Maltorian food once my new physiology took hold.

Jabbing the still writhing tentacle with a fork, I brought it up to my mouth and took a tentative bite. It was quite tasty; the only disturbing part was feeling it move around as it went down my throat.
11111111111111111111111111.title: Time for Dinner

111111111111111111111111111.content:
Asura and I went back upstairs to get dressed once dinner was over. While she went to finish some last-minute school assignment, I went downstairs to see if there was anything for me to do. Hearing some noises from the kitchen, I ventured down the hall to find Malura stacking the plates inside a matter reclaimer. “Do you need any help, Malura?”

“Thank you, Oliva; but I’ve got everything under control.” Pressing a button on the wall, she turned to me and said, “You’re more than welcome to stay and talk, if you’d like.”

“Are you sure?” Touched by the offer, I said, “I’m not sure I’d be good company right now.”

“Nonsense.” Gesturing to the nearby table, Malura said, “Have a seat and tell me what’s wrong.”

As the two of us sat at the table, I said, “Everyone I’ve met here has been incredibly supportive; especially you for taking me in.” sighing, I said, “I’m just not sure where I fit in anymore.”

“What do you mean?”

I looked at Malura as a single tear rolled down my cheek, “If and when I ever return to Earth, I’ll be a stranger to my own family. Sure, they’ll try to make me feel at home; but it won’t the same as before.” Sighing, I said, “Here on Maltor; I’ll look like everyone else, but I’ll still be a stranger in a foreign land.”

“You have nothing to worry about, Olivia.” Malura took my hands in hers, saying, “You won’t be alone here; every single person you’ve met so far will help you fit in.”

“You say that now; but who knows what the future will bring.”

Getting to her feet, still grasping my hands, Malura knelt in front of me, “I promise that you’ll never be alone, Olivia.” With a look on sincerity in her eyes, she said, “It’s true that no one knows what the future will bring. But I promise that whatever happens, I will always be here for you.”

Suddenly I was overwhelmed with emotion; tears pouring down my face as I hugged Malura, “Thank you.”

After waiting for me to stop crying, Malura took her seat once more. “I took some time off tomorrow to help you acclimate.”
Smiling, she continued, “What do you say we spend the day together?”

Wiping the tears from my eyes, I nodded and said, “I’d like that.”

“Excellent.” Getting up from the chair, Malura kissed me on the forehead and said, “Now why don’t you get ready for bed.”

“Okay.”
111111111111111111111111111.title: Talking with Malura

1111111111111111111111111111.content:
I didn’t have a problem falling asleep tonight; especially considering everything going on. The only problem I faced was finding a comfortable position due to the shape of my skull.

With that being said, I woke up shortly after midnight to probably the worst cramping I’d ever experienced. I let out a pained groan as I placed a hand on my stomach; as I found it difficult to speak.

My groaning must’ve awakened Asura, because I heard movement in the bunk below me. After a second, I saw her standing at my side with an annoyed look in her eyes. “Would you please be a little quieter, Olivia?”

Gritting my teeth in pain, I managed to say, “I don’t feel so good.”

The annoyed look on Asura's face was quickly replaced with concern, “What’s wrong?”

“I don’t know.” Groaning in pain once more, I said, “This is worse then any menstrual cramp I’ve ever had.”

Running towards the bedroom door, she said, “Don’t move; I’ll go get my parents.”

The pain was so intense that I lost all concept of time. Eventually Asura returned with her parents; Jervix holding a small device in his hands. Approaching me, he said, “Where does it hurt, Olivia?”

I touched the area where the pain was coming from, wincing in pain as I did. “It’s on my right side.”

With the device in hand, Jervix said, “I’m going to use this to scan your body.” As he waved the device over my side, he said, “I think I found the cause; one of your kidneys is in the process of absorbing the other one.”

Suddenly I experienced another wave of pain , this one worse then anything I’d ever felt. “Oh Chrestus, this hurts so much!”

Malura ran from the room; returning with the container Qu’ris had given me and a glass of neshta juice. Dumping a pill into her hand, she handed everything to me and said, “Take this; it’ll help with the pain.”

I quickly obliged and was surprised by how quickly the pill kicked in. After about a minute, the pain had subsided to a dull ache. Feeling better, I looked at everyone and said, “Thanks for the help, guys; I appreciate it.”

“You’re most welcome, Olivia.” Patting my shoulder, Jervix said, “Qu’ris warned us this would be painful for you. I’m glad we were here to help.”

Breathing a sigh of relief, I said, “If losing a kidney was this painful, I can only imagine how it’s going to feel when my heart gets larger.”

“Don’t worry about that; we’ve got plenty of painkillers for you.” Kissing my forehead, Malura said, “Now try to get some sleep; I’ve got a fun-filled day planned for us.”

Jervix and Malura left the room; leaving Asura and me alone. I never told her about what Malura and I discussed, “What was that all about?”

“Your mom took the day off to spend with me; to help me acclimate.” Looking at her, I said, “I’m sure she would’ve invited you, but you had school. You don’t have a problem with that, do you?”

“Of course not; I’d be pretty selfish if I did.” Smiling as she moved to get back in bed, Asura said, “Enjoy the free time while you can; because I heard my folks say they wanted to enroll you in school.”
1111111111111111111111111111.title: During the Night

11111111111111111111111111111.content:
I was up early the next morning, even though I had nowhere to go. After eating breakfast with the others, Malura and I watched as Jervix and Alura left for the day. A few minutes after they’d left, Malura turned to me and said, “So Olivia, what do you feel like doing today?”

Following her into the kitchen, I said, “I haven’t got a clue; in fact, I was kind of hoping we could stay here.”

“What?” A shocked look on her face, Malura said, “It’s a lovely day, Olivia; surely you’d have more fun if we went out.”

I glanced down at my bare feet; taking special notice of the fact that my little toes were starting to shrivel. Looking back at Malura, I said, “Honestly; I’m not sure I want people staring at me all day.” Shuffling my feet, I said, “I can’t say I’m human anymore; but at the same time, I’m not really Maltorian yet.”

Malura stepped out of the room for a moment; only to return holding something in her hands. “I thought you might say that; so I had Jervix synthesize this last night.”

Malura stepped closer so I could get a better look at what she was holding. I don’t know what it was – but it was about the size of a cellphone with a small display and some buttons on it. “What is it?”

“This device was originally created to show patients what they’d look like after reconstructive surgery.” Working some of the buttons on the device, she handed it to me and, “Why don’t you put it on your belt and try it out.”

I obliged and put it on my belt as Malura made a mirror appear on the wall. Turning to look at my reflection, I was astonished by what I saw. Looking back at me was a Maltorian girl - her skin a mixture of greens and blues. The reflection mirrored every move I made. Looking at the reflection, I couldn't shake the feeling that it was showing who I was meant to be. Turning to Malura, I said, “Is this what I’ll look like once everything is done?”

“No simulation is perfectly accurate, but I imagine you’ll be close to that.” Moving to stand next to me, she said, “Now there’s one more thing you may want to change before we go.”

“Oh?” confused, I said, “What’s that?”

“Your name; it’s close to a word for a particularly bad case of intestinal distress - ol’iva.”

I had to resist the urge to chuckle, “I suppose that explains why Asura and the others reacted the way they did when we first met.” looking at Malura, I said, “Do you have any suggestions?”

“It wouldn’t be appropriate for me to influence you in anyway; as whatever you choose will be your name for the rest of your life.”
Placing both her hands on my shoulders, Malura said, “However, if you can’t decide; we can simply tell people that you’re shy around new people.”-

Looking from Malura to my reflection in the mirror, I said, “I think I’d like to wait; perhaps I’ll make a list of potential names tonight.”
“Very well then.” Smiling, Malura said, “Now shall we depart?”
11111111111111111111111111111.title: Starting the Day

111111111111111111111111111111.content:
I didn’t think I was going to enjoy spending the day with Malura, but that attitude changed within the first hour of us leaving the house. After fueling up the runabout at a fusion station, Malura took me to a zoo on the outskirts of the city. I had thought a technologically advanced society like the Maltorians would have no need for zoos; until I realized each enclosure was almost 6 acres.

I saw creatures unlike anything I’d ever thought possible; including a pack of vulcits (a bear-like creature that stood on two legs) and klasians (cat-sized creatures that burrowed underground and spat rocks from its gullet in self-defense). There were dozens of other creatures as well, but those were the ones that I found to be the most interesting.

We toured the exhibits until about mid-day when Malura suggested we have lunch. We entered the enclosed food court; after removing our clothes, Malura went to order while I went to find us a place to sit.

After finding an empty booth, I tried to make myself as inconspicuous as possible so as not to draw attention. I was looking out the window at some of the scenery when I heard a commotion nearby. Turning in that direction, I saw a woman struggling to control a group of children. I figured they must’ve been here on a field trip, while noting Maltorian children could be just as hyper as humans.

I watched the kids for a few minutes until Malura arrived with the food; a no’kata wrap for each of us. I realized that I needed to expand my horizons when it came to food, but that was something for another day. After taking a bite, I looked at Malura and said, “Asura mentioned that you and Jervix were going to enroll me in school.”

“If you’re going to be living on Maltor, we thought you would benefit from a Maltorian education.” Putting her fork down, Malura looked at me, “Are you okay with that?”

I turned to look out the window for a moment, gathering my thoughts before responding. “I guess it makes sense to continue my education, especially if I end up being stuck here.” Sighing, turning back to Malura, I said, “But a part of me can’t help but wonder if that means I’ve given up on ever returning to Earth.”

“I certainly hope that you’ll return to Earth someday; but I’m reminded of a Terran idiom – hope for the best but prepare for the worst.” Reaching over to take my hand, Malura said, “If it makes you feel better, I’ll contact Qu’ris tonight to see if he’s heard anything.”

“Thanks, Malura.” With a weak smile, I said, “You’re really going above and beyond the call of duty.”

Nodding in acknowledgment, Malura said, “Chrestus teaches us to help those in need.”

************************


Malura and I left the foot court after an hour; I followed her as she approached a map of the area. Turning to me, Malura said, “Where do you feel like going next?”

I took one look at it before remembering that I couldn’t read Maltorian. “I’ll leave that up to you.”

“There’s a couple options in the immediate area we could try: an aquatic show or a special exhibit.”
111111111111111111111111111111.title: A Maltorian Zoo

1111111111111111111111111111112.content:
Malura and I walked through the zoo to a large building in the center of the property. As we walked towards the entrance, I noticed several of the kids from earlier were whining nearby – their chauffer looked as though he was less than thrilled. While Malura and I waited in line for entry into the building, I said, “Why are those kids so upset?”

“Due to the nature of the exhibit, you have to be over the age of 13 to enter.” Looking at me as we moved up in line, Malura said,
“I’d imagine the children are less than thrilled that they’ll be unable to see the exhibit.”

My curiosity was piqued, “What exactly is this exhibit about?”

“Terrans aren’t the only species that our kind has encountered. There’s another species called the Creel; their homeworld is located about 15 lightyears away.” Stopping briefly to look at me, Malura said, “Whereas our relationship with Terrans has been peaceful, we’ve been at war with the Creel for almost 40 years now.”

I noticed the emphasis Malura was putting on the word our; I considered questioning her about that until I reminded myself that I was becoming a Maltorian. So instead, I focused on the Creel, “Why would there be an exhibit about them if we’re at war with them?”

“Surely there are museums on Earth devoted to wars, and those involved in them.”

“There are loads of them, actually.” Sighing as we stepped ever closer to the door, I said, “In fact I’ve always thought Humans glorified the concept of war.”

It wasn’t long before Malura and I were next in line to enter. Once it was our turn, the two of us went inside and started looking around. A lot of the exhibits were what you’d expect; explaining who the Creel were and discussing the origins of the war. It seemed that fighting over resources wasn’t just limited to humans.

One of the last exhibits we came across was an enclosure holding about a dozen of the most hideous looking creatures I’d ever seen. They were like bulldogs in their size and weight, but that’s where the similarities ended. Their bodies were green in color, with three claws on each foot and sharp spikes protruding from their backs. I took special notice of the fact that they had two sets of eyes.

After watching one of them tear a carcass to shreds, I turned to Malura and said, “What are those things?”

“They’re called gharial, the Creel use them to hunt down any survivors.”

I was about to ask another question when I noticed several older Maltorians standing nearby, looking at the enclosure with tears in their eyes. “What’s got them upset?”

“They’ve probably lost loved ones to the Creel.” Malura looked around for a moment before pulling me into a nearby alcove, “You see; the gharial, and another species called the uakari, were once Maltorian.”

“What do you mean?”

“The Creel don’t just kill any Maltorians they come across; they also transform them to suit their needs.” Gesturing towards the enclosure, Malura said, “As far as those people are concerned, those gharial might have been their loved ones at some point.”

I stepped away from Malura, imagining how my family would feel if they saw the new me. “How is my situation any different from those things in that enclosure? I didn’t ask for this; and they sure as hell didn’t either.”
1111111111111111111111111111112.title: The Special Exhibit

11111111111111111111111111111121.content:
I was grateful that there weren’t that many people within earshot, as they were all focused on the gharial. “Olivia, your situation couldn’t be more different.”

“Really? Because I can’t see much difference where I’m standing.”

“It’s true that you didn’t ask to become Maltorian; but unlike all those gharial in there, you’ll still retain everything that makes you unique.” Taking a tentative step towards me, Malura said, “Everything that once made those creatures unique was destroyed the moment they were transformed. They’re nothing but primitive creatures now.”

I was all ready to respond when a commotion nearby caught my attention. Malura and I weren’t close enough to hear what was going on, but I could hear indistinct yelling in the distance. Both our interests piqued, Malura and I hurried to see what was going on.

Coming around the corner, I saw half a dozen Maltorians standing in front of the enclosure waving signs. I couldn’t read the signs, so I focused on what the protestors were saying. “We demand the immediate release of these creatures. They’re still Maltorian citizens and they have rights!”

Tugging on Malura’s sleeve, I said, “What’s going on?”

Leaning over to whisper, Malura said, “They’re part of a group who believes the gharial still retain remnants of their past selves. Even though studies have proven that to not be the case.” Looking at me, she continued, “They believe they can pressure the zoo’s leadership to release them.”

I watched as several armed guards made their way through the crowd; apprehending a few of the protestors. One of them, a female, got away and ran down a nearby ramp.
